Records.description: Two-loop triangle diagram with a massive bubble insertion, relevant for QCD corrections to heavy quark pair production. Has the equal-mass sunrise as a subtopology. One master integral in the top sector. Computed in $d=4-2\varepsilon$ with a dot on one massive propagator to ensure finiteness ($T_{1,2,1,1}$). The integral is expressed as a one-fold integral over a polylogarithmic integrand divided by $x$, with the elliptic curve arising from the equal-mass sunrise subtopology. Solved in terms of $E_4$-functions via canonical differential equations for the 6-master-integral IPP basis. Analytic continuation to the physical region validated against FIESTA.

One of the four example graphs of Fig. 3: a second two-loop three-point function depending on $x=p^2/m^2$, topologically distinct from figex3 but still built around the equal-mass sunrise subtopology and with at least one light-like external leg. Solid internal lines carry mass $m$, dashed internal lines are massless. As with the other Fig. 3 examples, the paper quotes the integral only graphically; explicit propagator momenta are not given, so propagators, edges, and external-vertex labels are reported as null. The Picard-Fuchs operator factorizes into first-order factors times the single second-order irreducible operator of the sunrise, so after the change of variable to the modular parameter $\tau$ and an appropriate rotation of the master-integral basis the differential equation is $\varepsilon$-factorized and the all-orders solution is given by iterated integrals of modular forms of $\Gamma_1(6)$.
Records.familyName: two-loop three-point function with one light-like leg containing equal-mass sunrise subtopology (example 4) / two-loop three-point function with one light-like leg containing equal-mass sunrise subtopology (example 3)
Records.location: Fig. 3 (figex4); Section 'Feynman integrals evaluating to iterated integrals of modular forms'

Records.description: One of the four example graphs of Fig. 3: a two-loop three-point function depending on the single dimensionless variable x=p^2/m^2, with at least one external dashed (light-like) leg, containing the equal-mass sunrise as a subtopology. Solid internal lines carry mass m, dashed internal lines are massless. No explicit propagator list is given in the paper, so propagators, edges, and external-vertex labels are reported as null. The same four-step strategy applies: verify via Picard-Fuchs factorization that only one elliptic curve appears (the sunrise curve), identify the curve, change variable x -> tau, and transform the master integrals so that the differential equation becomes eps-factorized, giving a solution to all orders in eps in terms of iterated integrals of modular forms of Gamma_1(6).
Records.familyName: two-loop three-point function with one light-like leg containing equal-mass sunrise subtopology (example 3)
Records.location: Fig. 3 (figex3); Section 'Feynman integrals evaluating to iterated integrals of modular forms'

Results.resultTeX: \frac{1}{2 \varepsilon^2}+\frac{\frac{3}{2}-\log (m^2)}{\varepsilon}+\left(\frac{2 H\left(0,\frac{1}{W_1^-+1}\,;1\right)}{W_1^-+1}+\frac{2 H\left(0,\frac{1}{W_1^++1}\,;1\right)}{W_1^++1}+\frac{\zeta(2) W_1^+}{\left(W_1^-+1\right) \left(W_1^++1\right)}+\frac{2 \zeta(2)}{\left(W_1^-+1\right) \left(W_1^++1\right)}+\frac{\zeta(2) W_1^-}{\left(W_1^-+1\right) \left(W_1^++1\right)}-\frac{2 \zeta(2)}{2 W_1^-+2}-\frac{\log ^2\left(-W_1^-\right)}{2 W_1^-+2}-\frac{2 \zeta(2)}{2 W_1^++2}-\frac{\log ^2\left(-W_1^+\right)}{2 W_1^++2}+\log ^2(m^2)-3 \log (m^2)+\frac{\zeta(2)}{2}+\frac{7}{2}\right)+O\left(\varepsilon^1\right)
Results.normalizedSymbolTeX: \begin{aligned}\varepsilon^{-2}&\colon\;\frac{1}{2} \\ \varepsilon^{-1}&\colon\;\frac{3}{2}-\log \left(m^2\right) \\ \varepsilon^{0}&\colon\;\frac{1}{\left(W_1^-+1\right) \left(W_1^++1\right)}\!\left[2 \left(W_1^++1\right)\,(W_1 \otimes W^-_1) - W_1^+-1\,(W^-_1 \otimes W^-_1) + 2 \left(W_1^-+1\right)\,(W_2 \otimes W^+_1) - W_1^--1\,(W^+_1 \otimes W^+_1)\right]\end{aligned}
